title: Markov Chains
description: Model systems with no memory: from weather prediction to PageRank. Build transition matrices, find stationary distributions, and discover why almost every Markov chain forgets its starting point — with concrete simulations at every step.
persona: For curious learners who finished Pattern Hitting Times and want to see the state-machine machinery in its full generality. Linear algebra intuition helpful but not required.
tagline: Where does a memoryless process settle?

lessons.title: The Markov Property
lessons.summary: Only the present matters: a memoryless process reads the current state and discards the path.

lessons.title: The Transition Matrix
lessons.summary: Stack each state's exits into a row of P — and every row sums to 1.

lessons.title: Multi-Step Transitions
lessons.summary: The chance of going i→j in n steps is one entry of Pⁿ — it sums every length-n path.

lessons.title: Classifying States
lessons.summary: Recurrent, transient, absorbing — structure, not arithmetic, decides where a chain ends up.

lessons.title: Hitting Times & Absorption
lessons.summary: One fundamental matrix (I−Q)⁻¹ prices both the absorption split and the time to a wall.

lessons.title: The Stationary Distribution
lessons.summary: The long-run share of time in each state is the unique π solving πP=π, Σπ=1.

lessons.title: Convergence
lessons.summary: A regular chain forgets its start: every row of Pⁿ marches to the same π.

lessons.title: Reversibility & Detailed Balance
lessons.summary: When every edge balances (πᵢpᵢⱼ=πⱼpⱼᵢ) the chain is reversible and π telescopes out.

lessons.title: PageRank
lessons.summary: A page's importance is its long-run share of a random surfer's visits — stationary π of the Google matrix.

lessons.title: Markov in the Wild
lessons.summary: One structural fork — absorbing or ergodic — picks the tool for every chain problem.
